description:
Fixes the idempotence principle (universal bug in the adherence review: eval outputs opened with mode 'w', silent overwrite of nondeterministic samples). Add step-level memoization to the Flow engine: cache key = hash(input artifact ids, params, step source code). On re-run, completed steps skip and return their persisted artifact — a crashed 200-job sweep resumes for free. Design points: (1) key MUST include the step's source hash or stale caches lie; (2) LLM-sampling steps get the honest semantics 're-run = replay persisted samples, refresh=True = new experiment'; (3) outputs stored via the artifacts service, which also enforces write-once persistence and closes the adoption gap. This makes stagehand a build system for experiments (cf. Make/Snakemake/Metaflow — content-keyed caching as core engine semantics, not framework bloat).

## Acceptance criteria
Re-running an identical Flow performs zero recomputation; changing a step's params or source re-runs that step and downstream only; refresh/cache=False opt-out exists; memoized outputs live in the artifacts service with lineage intact.

## Notes
Implemented on branch step-memo (stacked on run-manifest) → https://github.com/dtch1997/stagehand/pull/20 (v1.6.0, 126 tests pass). Key design: md5(fn source w/ closure recursion + static inputs + upstream result VALUES); JSON-only results; miss-never-wrong-hit; refresh=True re-records. Close on merge.
title: Content-keyed step memoization on the artifacts service
